perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term "Intellectual Property Rights" means know-how, inventions, patents, patent rights, and registrations and applications, renewals, continuations and extensions thereof, works of authorship and art, copyrightable materials and copyrights (including, but not limited to, titles, computer code, designs, themes, objects, characters, character names, stories, dialog, catch phrases, concepts, artwork, animation, sounds, musical compositions, graphics and visual elements, audio-visual effects and methods of operation, and any related documentation), copyright registrations and applications, renewals and extensions thereof, mask works, industrial rights, trademarks, service marks, trade names, logos, trademark registrations and applications, renewals and extensions thereof, trade secrets, rights in trade dress and packaging, publicity, personality and privacy rights, rights of attribution, paternity, integrity and other similarly afforded "moral" rights, and all other forms of intellectual property and proprietary rights recognized by the U.S. laws, and other applicable foreign and international laws, treaties and conventions.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term "Intellectual Assets" means know-how, inventions, patents, patent rights, and registrations and applications, renewals, continuations and extensions thereof, works of authorship and art, copyrightable materials and copyrights (including, but not limited to, titles, computer code, designs, themes, objects, characters, character names, stories, dialog, catch phrases, concepts, artwork, animation, sounds, musical compositions, graphics and visual elements, audio-visual effects and methods of operation, and any related documentation), copyright registrations and applications, renewals and extensions thereof, mask works, industrial rights, trademarks, service marks, trade names, logos, trademark registrations and applications, renewals and extensions thereof, trade secrets, rights in trade dress and packaging, publicity, personality and privacy rights, rights of attribution, paternity, integrity and other similarly afforded "moral" rights, and all other forms of intellectual property and proprietary rights recognized by the U.S. laws, and other applicable foreign and international laws, treaties and conventions.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "Intellectual Property Rights" to "Intellectual Assets" introduces an internal contradiction. The contract uses "Intellectual Property Rights" consistently throughout, and this single change creates uncertainty. It is unclear whether "Intellectual Assets" is intended to be the same as "Intellectual Property Rights" or if it refers to something different, thus creating confusion on what the contractual agreement encompases.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Fox shall own all Intellectual Property Rights in and to any derivative works made from the Properties, whether or not used in the Wireless Products, including without limitation design documents, graphics, animation, music, packaging, advertising, promotional and other artwork used in connection with the development and distribution of the Wireless Products but at all times excluding the Licensee Materials as defined in Paragraph 11(c) below (collectively, the "Fox Intellectual Property"). The Fox Intellectual Property includes all rights in and to the entire "look and feel" of the Wireless Products, all visual displays, scripts, dialogue, literary treatments, concepts, characters, backgrounds, environments, and other elements visible to the Wireless Products' users; all sounds, sound effects, soundtracks and other elements audible to the user. Licensee acknowledges and agrees Fox shall be the exclusive owner of these rights as a work made for hire. Licensee acknowledges that Licensee's use of any Property shall not confer or imply a grant of rights, title or interest in the Property or good will associated therewith, except as specifically provided herein.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Intellectual Property Rights' to 'Intellectual Assets' creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted text, which refers to 'Fox Intellectual Property,' explicitly uses the term 'Intellectual Property Rights' when defining what Fox owns. The changed definition raises uncertainty as to whether 'Intellectual Assets' and 'Intellectual Property Rights' are intended to be interchangeable, thus making the scope of the Fox Intellectual Property unclear and creating a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction as described in the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: Licensee shall pay to Fox a non-refundable guarantee in the amount of US$***** ("Guarantee"), inclusive of the Advance. The Guarantee shall be recoupable against royalty payments and payable as follows: (a) US$***** inclusive of the Advance above; (b) US$***** on or before *****; (c) US$***** on or before *****; (d) US$***** on or before *****; (e) US$***** on or before *****; (f) US$***** on or before *****; and (g) US$***** on or before *****. There shall be no cross-collateralization among the Royalties earned, as defined in Paragraph 7 below, for the purposes of recouping the Guarantee for any of the Major Releases or Targeted Releases.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee shall pay to Fox a non-refundable deposit in the amount of US$***** ("Deposit"), inclusive of the Advance. The Deposit shall be recoupable against royalty payments and payable as follows: (a) US$***** inclusive of the Advance above; (b) US$***** on or before *****; (c) US$***** on or before *****; (d) US$***** on or before *****; (e) US$***** on or before *****; (f) US$***** on or before *****; and (g) US$***** on or before *****. There shall be no cross-collateralization among the Royalties earned, as defined in Paragraph 7 below, for the purposes of recouping the Guarantee for any of the Major Releases or Targeted Releases.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Guarantee" to "Deposit" in section 6(a), but keeping "Guarantee" in the last sentence, introduces a contradiction. The rest of the contract refers to the payment as a "Guarantee." Now, it is ambiguous whether the no cross-collateralization clause applies to the "Deposit" or only to something called "Guarantee" which is not defined, making the clause unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Individual Property Guarantees: Notwithstanding the payment schedule set forth in Paragraph 6(a) above, a minimum recoupable guarantee (each "Individual Property Guarantee") shall be apportioned from the Guarantee set forth in Paragraph 6(a) above to each of the individual Major Releases and Targeted Releases set forth on Exhibit A as follows for the purposes of determining recoupment of Guarantees and Royalty payments attributable to each of the Major Releases and Targeted Releases as set forth in Paragraph 7 below: (i) Major Releases: The Individual Property Guarantee for each of the Major Releases set forth on Exhibit A shall be US$*****. (ii) Targeted Releases: The Individual Property Guarantee for each of the Targeted Releases set forth on Exhibit A shall be US$*****.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined a payment as a 'Guarantee', and subsequent sections, including the contradicted text, rely on this definition. The changed text modifies this to 'Deposit' but retains the term 'Guarantee' elsewhere in the same section (6(a)). This introduces ambiguity, as the contradicted text (b) relies on the definition of 'Guarantee' from 6(a). Because 6(a) now defines the initial payment as a 'Deposit' while the last sentence in 6(a) and the contradicted text both refer to a 'Guarantee', it is unclear if the terms are interchangeable, or if the obligations related to 'Guarantee' are still applicable to the 'Deposit'. This creates an in-text contradiction, as per the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' which states that using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of termination of this Agreement and without limitation of Fox's rights and remedies all of which are expressly reserved, the following payment penalties shall apply: (i) if termination occurs in the first year of the Term, Licensee shall forfeit any Guarantee paid, and shall immediately pay any remaining Guarantee, up to the amount of ***** dollars (US$*****), and any Guarantee payments made in excess of ***** dollars ($*****) shall be refunded by Fox to Licensee; and (ii) if termination occurs in the second year of the Term, Licensee shall forfeit any Guarantee paid, and shall immediately pay any remaining portion of the Guarantee then unpaid, up to the amount of ***** dollars (US$*****). Any forfeited Guarantees will apply as a set off against any additional damages claimed by Fox.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of termination of this Agreement and without limitation of Fox's rights and remedies all of which are expressly reserved, the following payment penalties shall apply: (i) if termination occurs in the first year of the Term, Licensee shall forfeit any Deposit paid, and shall immediately pay any remaining Deposit, up to the amount of ***** dollars (US$*****), and any Deposit payments made in excess of ***** dollars ($*****) shall be refunded by Fox to Licensee; and (ii) if termination occurs in the second year of the Term, Licensee shall forfeit any Deposit paid, and shall immediately pay any remaining portion of the Deposit then unpaid, up to the amount of ***** dollars (US$*****). Any forfeited Guarantees will apply as a set off against any additional damages claimed by Fox.
perturbation.explanation: Following the previous change, this perturbation keeps the inconsistency alive. The first two lines uses Deposit, but the last line states the forfeited *Guarantees* will apply as a set off against any additional damages claimed by Fox. Again, like the previous perturbation, this creates ambiguity because of the differing terminology withing the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensee shall pay to Fox a non-refundable deposit in the amount of US$***** ("Deposit"), inclusive of the Advance. The Deposit shall be recoupable against royalty payments and payable as follows: (a) US$***** inclusive of the Advance above; (b) US$***** on or before *****; (c) US$***** on or before *****; (d) US$***** on or before *****; (e) US$***** on or before *****; (f) US$***** on or before *****; and (g) US$***** on or before *****. There shall be no cross-collateralization among the Royalties earned, as defined in Paragraph 7 below, for the purposes of recouping the Guarantee for any of the Major Releases or Targeted Releases.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Guarantee' with 'Deposit' in the payment penalties section. However, the last sentence of the changed text still refers to 'forfeited Guarantees'. The contradicted text defines 'Deposit' and outlines its recoupment against royalties. By using both 'Deposit' and 'Guarantee' interchangeably within the same section (the changed text), and especially because it makes reference to setting off 'Guarantees', it creates a direct contradiction as the contradicted text refers to how the 'Deposit' will be paid and that there is no cross-collateralization. The ambiguous use of terms violates the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
